puzzle.title: The Trial Beneath the Waves
puzzle.intro: A prophet, burdened by his mission, made a decision that led him far from his people and into an unexpected trial. His journey took a dramatic turn, testing his faith and patience in the most profound way. Can you deduce the details of this extraordinary event from the clues provided?
clues: A prophet was sent to a people, but departed in haste, feeling frustration with their rejection.
His journey led him to the sea, where he boarded a heavily laden vessel seeking passage.
A great storm arose, threatening the ship, and a decision was made to cast one person overboard to lighten the load, determined by lots.
He was swallowed by a creature of the deep, a miraculous event decreed by Divine will.
Within the confines of the creature's belly, he called out to his Lord, repenting and glorifying Allah in the darkness.
After a period of intense supplication, he was cast upon a barren shore, weak but alive, and later returned to find his people had finally believed.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The journey in the belly of the great fish
trial.answer: The journey in the belly of the great fish

outcome.label: Outcome
options: A miraculous birth for his people
A kingdom established for his lineage
A return to his people who then believed
A victory over a tyrannical ruler
outcome.answer: A return to his people who then believed

puzzle.verse: And [mention] Dhun-Nun, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. But he called out within the darknesses, 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.' (Al-Anbiya 21:87)
puzzle.arabic: وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَٰضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
